description_long: This is a single-story green bungalow with a prominent gable roof covered in gray shingles. The front facade features a small set of wooden steps leading to a central door, flanked by two symmetrically placed windows. On one side, there is a small covered entryway or porch extension, providing a sheltered entrance. The siding is horizontal and painted a muted green, with white trim around the windows and roof edges. The rear and side elevations show additional windows, including a smaller window in the gable peak. The overall design is compact and functional, typical of early to mid-20th-century American residential architecture.
description: A green, single-story bungalow with a gray gable roof, white trim, and a small covered entryway. Symmetrical windows and a central door define its classic, compact design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the central door, two windows, and a small covered porch extension on the right.
description_view_1: Side gable view with two lower windows and a single upper window in the gable peak, no porch visible.
description_view_2: Direct front view with the central door and two symmetrically placed windows, porch extension less visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side gable view with two lower windows and a single upper window, porch extension visible in the background.
